topics: Overview of a Database Project and Its Scope and Objectives
Importance of Database Management System in Real-World Applications
Create an Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD) to Model the Entities, Relationships, and Attributes Involved in Your Project
Translate the ERD into a Relational Schema
Implement a Sample Schema in a DBMS of Your Choice (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite)
Ensure Your Schema Is Normalized to Minimize Redundancy and Dependency Issues
Develop a Set of SQL Queries to Demonstrate CRUD Operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete), Complex Joins, Aggregations, and Any Specialized Queries Required by Your Project
Create a Timeline or Project Plan with Milestones to Ensure Timely Completion
Definition of Transactions and Transaction Properties (ACID Properties)
Techniques to Manage Concurrent Access (e.g., Locking, Time Stamping)
Strategies for Database Backup and Recovery in Case of Failures
Execute Queries to Validate the Correctness of the Schema and Constraints
Develop Simple Applications (e.g., Using Python) to Interact with the Database
Prepare Comprehensive Documentation Covering Project Objectives, Database Schema, Constraints, Transactions, and Implementation Details
